fields_json: {"name":"Rebecca Price","bio":"Board Certified Hospital Chaplain at Bluff City Medical Center and host of Morning Reflections with Rebecca on WGSP 1490 AM. Rebecca writes from the corner of faith, community, and everyday life in Memphis, TN.","twitter":"@morningreflectionsrebecca"}
{"name":"Rev. Angela Dawson","bio":"Chaplain at Golden Heart Medical Center and ordained minister. Angela writes on faith in healthcare settings and clergy care.","twitter":""}

{"name":"Pastor William Greer","bio":"Senior Pastor of Grace Community Church in Memphis, TN. Pastor Greer has served the Memphis community for over 40 years and contributes occasional reflections on scripture and congregational life.","twitter":""}

{"title":"Setting Intention for the New Year: A Chaplain's Perspective","slug":"setting-intention-new-year","body":"Every January I ask myself the same question I ask patients on their hardest days: what do you most need right now? That question doesn't shrink for a new year - it expands. Intention is not a resolution. It's a direction. Pick one word, one practice, one relationship you want to tend more carefully in the coming months. That's enough. That's a start.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-rebecca"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-new-year"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Show Notes: January 4, 2026 - Beginnings","slug":"show-notes-jan-4-2026","body":"This week on Morning Reflections we opened 2026 with a conversation about what it means to begin again. Segment one featured a reading from Howard Thurman's 'The Mood of Christmas.' Segment two: listener questions on finding stillness in a noisy January. Music included Lauren Daigle's 'You Say' and a classic Keith & Kristyn Getty hymn. Thanks to our sponsors Covenant Insurance Group, Mitchell's Flowers, and Bluff City Auto Care for keeping this show on the air.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-rebecca"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-studio-morning"}},"published":true}

{"title":"When Caring Wears You Down: Recognizing Compassion Fatigue","slug":"recognizing-compassion-fatigue","body":"Caregivers - nurses, chaplains, social workers, teachers, parents - often pour out more than they take in. Compassion fatigue is not weakness. It is the cost of loving people through hard things. The antidote is not caring less. It's learning to receive. Rest is not a reward you earn after you've given everything. Rest is what makes giving possible.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-rebecca"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-care"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Show Notes: February 1, 2026 - Love and Loss","slug":"show-notes-feb-1-2026","body":"February always brings love to the forefront. This week we explored what it means to love well through grief and change. Guest Rev. Angela Dawson joined to share reflections on pastoral care and belonging. Playlist featured Chris Tomlin and Brandi Carlile. Full episode available on the WGSP 1490 AM website.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-rebecca"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-studio-morning"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Rooted in Memphis: Faith, Freedom, and Black History","slug":"rooted-in-memphis-faith-freedom","body":"Memphis is not just a city - it is a testimony. The people who built this place, who sang in its churches and marched on its streets, carried faith as both anchor and propulsion. Black History Month is not a retrospective exercise. It is a reminder of who we are and what we are called to continue. I am grateful every Saturday morning to speak on a frequency that has carried Black voices for generations.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-rebecca"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-memphis"}},"published":true}

{"title":"New Year Sunrise: A Meditation on Beginning Again","slug":"easter-sunrise-meditation-2026","body":"Every New Year sunrise service I am asked to offer something short - three minutes or so before the light fully crests the horizon. This year I kept coming back to one phrase: the darkness could not hold it. Not just one story of grace, but every moment of light that defies the odds. You've had your own long nights. You know what it is to sit in that dark and wonder. This morning, I want to offer you permission to step into the light - however tentative that first step is.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-rebecca"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-sunrise"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Honoring the Women of Faith on Morning Reflections: Celebrating All Kinds of Mothers","slug":"mothers-day-morning-reflections-2026","body":"Honoring the women of faith lands differently for everyone. Some of us carry joy, some carry grief, and many carry both at once. On this week's show we'll be opening the phone lines and welcoming stories from Memphis mothers, daughters, and those honoring mothers they've lost. Tune in to WGSP 1490 AM, Saturday January 10, 7 to 9 AM. And if you can't catch it live, show notes and highlights post here each week.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-rebecca"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-mothers-day"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Grace Community Church Community Health Fair - Save the Date","slug":"grace-church-health-fair-2026","body":"On Saturday, October 3, 2026, Grace Community Church will once again host our annual Health Fair in partnership with Bluff City Community College's nursing program. Free screenings, wellness resources, and community connection - all in one place. Deacon Charles Mosley and our faithful volunteers have been planning this for months. Mark your calendars and spread the word.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-rebecca"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-health-fair"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Show Notes: January 16, 2026 - Midwinter Grace","slug":"show-notes-jun-14-2026","body":"This week we leaned into the quiet of a Memphis winter with a listener appreciation segment and a special dedication hour. Caller highlights included a longtime WGSP listener from Whitehaven celebrating 40 years of marriage. Playlist featured Patsy Cline, James Taylor, and a contemporary worship set. Schedule note: next week's episode will be pre-recorded - I'll be at Sandra's for a quiet evening where the real warmth is.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-rebecca"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-studio-morning"}},"published":true}

{"title":"What CPE Taught Me That Seminary Couldn't","slug":"what-cpe-taught-me","body":"Draft - CPE residency at Regional One changed everything I thought I knew about presence. Seminary gave me the theology. Clinical Pastoral Education gave me the silence. Still working through how to write about this without making it about me.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-rebecca"}},"published":false}

{"title":"A Conversation with Pastor William Greer: 40 Years of Pastoral Ministry in Memphis","slug":"pastor-greer-40-years","body":"Pastor William Greer has been a voice of steadiness in Memphis for four decades. When I asked him what he's learned after forty years of ministry, he didn't talk about sermons or programs. He talked about showing up. 'The presence itself is the ministry,' he said. I've carried those words into every hospital room I've ever entered. This week on Morning Reflections we rebroadcast our conversation with Pastor Greer - a reminder that faithfulness accumulates.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-rebecca"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-hero-pastor-greer"}},"published":true}
